I think there’s a bit of a cultural tendency to talk about parenting as though permissiveness is a solution to punitiveness or vice versa. And I think this is a false dichotomy and we need a better way to talk about Kids These Days.
So let me give you an example:

A few months ago I was in a brewery reading, as is my wont, and there was a toddler walking around interacting with things she found while her guardian followed her. The toddler picked up my book and started to walk off with it. Her guardian said, “can she play with it?” to me.

Now, I knew there was a zero percent chance my paperback was surviving a lap around a bar with a toddler, so I said no. Kid gave it back. No one was rude. No hard feelings. 
Now: I have literally no negative feelings about this lady. I saw her one time. I have no idea if she

had slept an hour and cringed herself into a backache realizing she’d asked a stranger to let her kid play with their novel in public. Or maybe she’s self absorbed and thought I was a bitch. I don’t care. 
The book is fine. Nothing happened.

But here’s the thing: I think a lot of people would hear that story and say “well that’s why you need to spank your kid, so they don’t go around picking up things that aren’t theirs! She is going to grow up to be entitled!”
But I don’t think that fixes anything.

Let’s say your two year old is in public. They pick up the centerpiece, a bottle of ketchup, and a lady’s book. You let them pick up the centerpiece, look really anxious when she picks up the ketchup, and hit her when she picks up the lady’s book.

I have a hard time believing the two year old learns more from that if you let her pick up all three. Does a two year old really know the difference between a centerpiece and a lady’s book? How? Or does this just seem like random chaotic consequences?

But that’s not a goal you can have for your kid in public- “not these three things.” For a little brain it has to feel hopelessly abstract. 
But I don’t think “longer leash, shorter leash” or “harsh consequences less harsh consequences” really resolves that.

I think the issue is that we don’t really have a positive vision of what kids should be doing in public and so managing them in public is kind of fighting the wind. We know they shouldn’t run around, yell, break things, and depending who you ask, be on screens.

I didn’t even know about the GameScoops shit for years.

That said, my issue with how critics criticized Sonic was how arbitrary it was.  

It sounded less like critiques and more like oldheads nitpicking shit because boohoo, Sonic evolved past basic 2D side scrolling. https://t.co/twitter_link

And granted, some people just prefer that and that’s fine. Everyone has their preferences. I think Archie Sonic is the greatest faction of the franchise.

But when the conversation around Sonic is “Serious stories bad” “Sonic had a rough transition in 3D”

“Sonic and his stupid friends” “Sonic found his footing in Sonic Colors”

Then it comes off as everyone had out for Sonic for what defined the franchise in the jump to 3D. 

Because Sonic wasn’t some goofy, minimalistic saturday morning cartoon platformer like Mario.

Even when you look back at the GameScoops video, they were excited for Mania and then went to trash Forces for being “dark” “gritty” and “reeks of werehog” and mind you, this was right after the 25th Anniversary party so this was when it was Project Sonic 2017

With the CGI teaser. And they used that as a jumping off point to say Sonic was never good and trash it and apparently thinking we want to fuck Big The Cat

There is a VERY CLEAR bias with what the critics want. Yes, I understand them being burned so many times

Granted, he said he did enjoy Frontiers so maybe it’s not complete bias with him

But eversince Colors, the narrative has been 3D Sonic sucks, Sonic doesn’t need to be ambitious, Sonic shouldn’t have multiple playable characters, Sonic shouldn’t take itself seriously

And I personally get it, hell, I’m trying to find something myself that can take over Sonic for me so I can leave this fuck ass franchise and fanbase behind if the next 3D outing is mediocre.

But this starts from a deeper issue of bias about what they want Sonic to be.

Lots of posts about how there isn't enough color/variation/creativity in the Freman clothing and agree with that. Worms are ... not scary enough in part two, which is a shame to me, bc I love the shot from part one with the Huge Worm just hovering over Paul like he's watching him

Rewatching the first movie last night to watch part two this morning did make me think about how much time I spend with Paul&Jessica (amazing, 10/10), but also that I would love to read something that digs into Paul&Leto as well.
Is The Boys good? I’m literally foaming at the mouth to find anything remotely good to watch. 😭
Also this is a global prestige ranking. Not based on one or two years of success or failure. Largely based on where I see them in the global prestige picture around the world.
